steps.title: Connect omission to inaccuracy
steps.content: Mercer argued that the USGS left out important considerations when determining Nebraska's geographic center, including Earth's curvature. The next sentence concludes that the USGS-determined center was inaccurate because of those omissions.

steps.title: Rule out noncausal signals
steps.content: A marks a sequence of events, but the passage needs a logical consequence. B marks concession, and D marks contrast, both of which would weaken the connection between omitted factors and inaccuracy. The transition should show that the inaccuracy follows from Mercer's criticism. The answer is C.
